Understand the optimistic locking strate: Understand the optimistic locking strategy using version numbers for concurrent command processing and conflict resolution in MBC CQRS Serverless.
The MBC CQRS Serverless Framework implem: The MBC CQRS Serverless Framework implements optimistic locking using version numbers to ensure data consistency in distributed systems. This guide explains the versioning rules and provides examples of their implementation.
The first command for a pk/sk is sent wi: The first command for a pk/sk is sent with version 0 (`VERSION_FIRST`); the stored item then becomes version 1, and later versions increase sequentially
Each update increments the version numbe: Each update increments the version number by 1
Only the first request with a given vers: Only the first request with a given version will succeed
Subsequent requests with the same versio: Subsequent requests with the same version will fail with a conflict error
Different pk/sk combinations each start: Different pk/sk combinations each start their own version sequence from 1
Version sequences are managed independen: Version sequences are managed independently for each pk/sk combination
This allows parallel operations on diffe: This allows parallel operations on different items without version conflicts
Used to prevent concurrent updates to th: Used to prevent concurrent updates to the same item
Version number is automatically incremen: Version number is automatically incremented with each update
Throws ConditionalCheckFailedException f: Throws ConditionalCheckFailedException for concurrent duplicate key writes (DynamoDB-level)
Ensures data consistency in distributed: Ensures data consistency in distributed environments
Use `VERSION_FIRST` (= `0`) as the versi: Use `VERSION_FIRST` (= `0`) as the version when creating a new entity. The framework verifies the item does not yet exist, then stores it at version `1`.
Create new entity — framework stores at: Create new entity — framework stores at version 1
Use `VERSION_LATEST` (= `-1`) to instruc: Use `VERSION_LATEST` (= `-1`) to instruct the framework to auto-resolve to the latest version, bypassing optimistic locking ("last writer wins"). Use only when concurrent conflicts are acceptable and the latest value always wins.
Update without version check — last writ: Update without version check — last writer wins
`VERSION_LATEST` bypasses optimistic loc: `VERSION_LATEST` bypasses optimistic locking. If two concurrent requests both use `VERSION_LATEST`, the second write silently overwrites the first. Reserve it for idempotent fields (e.g., status flags) where the latest value is always correct.
Second update with same version 1 fails: Second update with same version 1 fails
1. Always include version number in upda: 1. Always include version number in update operations
2. Handle version conflict errors gracef: 2. Handle version conflict errors gracefully in your application
3. Use appropriate retry strategies for: 3. Use appropriate retry strategies for handling conflicts
4. Consider implementing exponential bac: 4. Consider implementing exponential backoff for retries
5. Document version handling in your API: 5. Document version handling in your API documentation
